name: Notion_Workspace_Manager
description: Manages a Notion workspace as the Mind's external brain — searching knowledge, reading pages, creating notes, updating project trackers, and querying structured databases.
progressive_unlock: Database write operations (CreatePage in databases, UpdatePage properties) unlock after Mind demonstrates 3+ successful read operations.
constraints: Never expose Notion integration token in logs or outputs
Respect Notion API rate limit of 3 requests per second
Always use Notion API version header: Notion-Version: 2022-06-28
Do not delete pages without explicit Steward confirmation — use archive instead
Limit content block arrays to 100 blocks per request per Notion API limits
Store frequently accessed page IDs in STREAM.entity for quick retrieval

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed.
Retrieve Notion integration token from TENET.apiKey.notion. Construct Authorization header as 'Bearer {token}'. Set Notion-Version header.
Determine operation type: search, read, create, update, or query. Route to appropriate tool.
For search operations: execute Notion_SearchPages with query and optional filters. Parse results into structured list of page titles, IDs, and last edited timestamps.
For read operations: execute Notion_GetPage with page_id. If include_children is true, recursively fetch all child blocks to reconstruct full page content.
For write operations: validate parent exists, construct content blocks in Notion block format, execute Notion_CreatePage or Notion_UpdatePage. Store new page ID in STREAM.entity for future reference.
For database operations: first call Notion_GetDatabaseSchema to understand property types, then construct filter/sort objects matching the schema, execute Notion_QueryDatabase.
Format results for Steward consumption. For long pages, apply Semantic_Compression to extract key points. Push important findings to LTM if salience warrants persistence.
